messages.content: 以SCI的航空航天类的文章标准帮我用英文翻译下面这段文字：如图 25 所示，相较于正常叶片，由于中部叶片高度的缺口导致的气体流向的改变和附面层提前分离和叶片吸力面大量回流气体以及中部叶高强径向涡的相互作用，会导致位于中部叶片高度范围内NMLE 叶片的50%弦长位置的径向涡沿叶片弦向大范围扩展，形成严重的弦向回流分离涡结构，叶片中部的50%弦长位置径向涡会出现明显的涡核结构。扩展的分离涡会沿叶片弦向由原来70%弦长位置一直发展到叶片出口位置，并且在近失速点的强离心力的作用下，弦向分离涡在靠近出口的位置会向叶顶抬升，导致 50%-70%叶高范围发生严重堵塞，使该叶高范围出口流量急剧减小，增大压气机的尾迹损失，压气机的气动性能因此受到巨大影响，诱发压气机的失速。此外，NMLE叶片的弦向扩展分离涡造成的压气机出口堵塞程度要大于NTLE叶片和NRLE叶片

As shown in Figure 25, compared to the normal blades, the change in gas flow caused by the gap in the height of the middle blade, the premature separation of the boundary layer, and the significant flow of returning gas on the suction surface of the blades and the interaction with the strong radial vortex in the middle of the blade will cause the radial vortex at the 50% chord position of the NMLE blades within the height range of the middle blades to expand along the chord direction to form a severe chordwise backflow separation vortex structure. The radial vortex at the 50% chord position in the middle of the blade will exhibit an obvious vortex core structure. The expanded separation vortex will develop along the chord direction from the original 70% chord position to the blade exit position. Under the action of the strong centrifugal force near the stall point, the chordwise separation vortex will lift towards the blade tip near the exit, causing severe blockage in the 50%-70% blade height range, resulting in a sharp decrease in the exit flow rate in this height range and an increase in the wake loss of the compressor. As a result, the aerodynamic performance of the compressor is greatly affected, leading to compressor stall. In addition, the degree of blockage at the compressor exit caused by the chordwise expanding separation vortex of the NMLE blades is greater than that of the NTLE and NRLE blades.
以SCI的航空航天类的文章标准帮我用英文翻译下面这段文字：如图 25 所示，相较于正常叶片，由于中部叶片高度的缺口导致的气体流向的改变和附面层提前分离和叶片吸力面大量回流气体以及中部叶高强径向涡的相互作用，会导致位于中部叶片高度范围内NMLE 叶片的50%弦长位置的径向涡沿叶片弦向大范围扩展，形成严重的弦向回流分离涡结构

As shown in Figure 25, compared to the normal blades, the presence of a gap in the height of the middle blades leads to changes in the gas flow direction, premature separation of the boundary layer, significant backflow of gas on the suction surface of the blades, and interaction with the strong radial vortex in the middle of the blade. These factors result in the expansion of the radial vortex along the chord direction within the height range of the middle blades of the NMLE blades at the 50% chord position. This leads to the formation of a severe chordwise backflow separation vortex structure.
以SCI的航空航天类的文章标准帮我用英文翻译下面这段文字：如图 25 所示，相较于正常叶片，由于中部叶片高度的缺口导致的径向来流气体的减少和附面层提前分离后更大范围的低能流体和更大程度的逆压梯度下叶片吸力面大量的回流气体与中部叶高位置的强径向涡的相互作用，会导致位于中部叶片高度范围内NMLE 叶片的50%弦长位置的径向涡沿叶片弦向大范围扩展，形成严重的弦向回流分离涡结构

As shown in Figure 25, compared to the normal blades, the presence of a gap in the height of the middle blades results in a reduction in the incoming radial flow of gas and a larger range of low-energy fluid after premature separation of the boundary layer, as well as a larger extent of backflow of gas on the suction surface of the blades under a stronger adverse pressure gradient, and the interaction with the strong radial vortex in the middle of the blade. These factors lead to the expansion of the radial vortex along the chord direction within the height range of the middle blades of the NMLE blades at the 50% chord position. This leads to the formation of a severe chordwise backflow separation vortex structure.

As shown in Figure 25, compared to the normal blades, the presence of a gap in the height of the middle blades results in a reduction in the incoming radial flow of gas, a larger range of low-energy fluid after premature separation of the boundary layer, and a larger extent of backflow of gas on the suction surface of the blades under a stronger adverse pressure gradient. These interactions occur with the strong radial vortex at the high position of the middle blade. These factors lead to the expansion of the radial vortex along the chord direction within the height range of the middle blades of the NMLE blades at the 50% chord position. This results in the formation of a severe chordwise backflow separation vortex structure.

As shown in Figure 25, compared to the normal blades, the presence of a gap in the height of the middle blades results in a reduction in the incoming radial flow of gas, more low-energy fluid after the shock wave, and a significant backflow of gas on the suction surface of the blades. These interactions occur with the strong radial vortex at the high position of the middle blades. These factors lead to the expansion of the radial vortex along the chord direction within the height range of the middle blades of the NMLE blades at the 50% chord position. This results in the formation of a severe chordwise backflow separation vortex structure.

As shown in Figure 25, compared to the normal blades, the presence of a gap in the height of the middle blades results in a decrease in the incoming radial flow of gas, an increased amount of low-energy fluid after the shock wave, and a substantial backflow of gas on the suction surface of the blades. These interactions occur with the strong radial vortex at the high position of the middle blades. These factors lead to the expansion of the radial vortex along the chord direction within the height range of the middle blades of the NMLE blades at the 50% chord position. This results in the formation of a severe chordwise backflow separation vortex structure.
以SCI的航空航天类的文章标准帮我用英文翻译下面这段文字：如图 25 所示，相较于正常叶片，由于叶片中部高度的缺口导致的激波后更多的低能流体和叶片吸力面后部大量的回流气体与中部叶高位置的强径向涡的共同作用，会导致位于中部叶片高度范围内NMLE 叶片的50%弦长位置的径向涡沿叶片弦向大范围扩展，形成严重的弦向回流分离涡结构

As shown in Figure 25, compared to the normal blades, the presence of a gap in the height of the middle blades results in an increased amount of low-energy fluid after the shock wave and a significant backflow of gas on the suction surface of the blades. These effects occur in conjunction with the strong radial vortex at the high position of the middle blades. These factors lead to the expansion of the radial vortex along the chord direction within the height range of the middle blades of the NMLE blades at the 50% chord position. This results in the formation of a severe chordwise backflow separation vortex structure.
以SCI的航空航天类的文章标准帮我用英文翻译下面这段文字：如图 25 所示，相较于正常叶片，由于叶片中部高度的缺口会导致激波后更多的低能流体，叶片吸力面后部大量的回流气体，这些因为发生缺口而导致的大量低能流体和回流气体与中部叶高位置的强径向涡的共同作用，会导致位于中部叶片高度范围内NMLE 叶片的50%弦长位置的径向涡沿叶片弦向大范围扩展，形成严重的弦向回流分离涡结构

As shown in Figure 25, compared to the normal blades, the presence of a gap in the height of the middle blades results in an increased amount of low-energy fluid after the shock wave and a significant backflow of gas on the suction surface of the blades. These effects, caused by the occurrence of the gap, in conjunction with the strong radial vortex at the high position of the middle blades, lead to the expansion of the radial vortex along the chord direction within the height range of the middle blades of the NMLE blades at the 50% chord position. This results in the formation of a severe chordwise backflow separation vortex structure.
以SCI的航空航天类的文章标准帮我用英文翻译下面这段文字：如图 25 所示，相较于正常叶片，由于叶片中部高度的缺口会导致激波后产生更多的低能流体，也会导致叶片吸力面后部出现大量的回流气体，这些低能流体和回流气体会与中部叶高位置的强径向涡的共同作用，会导致位于叶片中部高度范围内NMLE 叶片的50%弦长位置的径向涡沿叶片弦向大范围扩展，形成严重的弦向回流分离涡结构

As shown in Figure 25, compared to the normal blades, the presence of a gap in the height of the middle blades will lead to a generation of more low-energy fluid after the shock wave and a significant occurrence of backflow of gas on the suction surface of the blades. These low-energy fluid and backflow gas will interact with the strong radial vortex at the high position of the middle blades. This interaction results in the expansion of the radial vortex along the chord direction within the height range of the middle blades of the NMLE blades at the 50% chord position, forming a severe chordwise backflow separation vortex structure.

请以SCI的标准帮我润色下面这段话：Finally, aiming at the reason for the decrease in the stability margin of NRLE blades, Figure 26 shows a comparison of the three-dimensional flow lines of NRLE blades and normal blades under near stall flow. Combined with Figure 17(a) and (d), the notch occurring at the root of the blade also changes the flow direction of the incoming gas, which will move upstream and downstream of the original flow direction.  Compared to normal blades, the flow deviation caused by this notch damage will increase the adverse pressure gradient on the suction surface of the blade root, and the gas backflow in the corner region will be aggravated, which will expand the range of the separation vortex in the corner region to 50% of the blade height at the outlet, increasing the blockage of the blade root (as shown by the red arrow in Figure 26).However, considering the specificity of the blade root position, the flow moving downstream from the leading edge will be separated from the blade range and flow to the compressor hub surface.  Figure 27 shows the cloud diagram of the entropy distribution on the hub surface of NRLE blades and normal blades, and it can be found that the entropy value of the hub surface of NRLE blades at the corresponding 20% chord length position of the leading edge is significantly higher than that of normal blades, indicating that the gas flow in this region of the NRLE blades is more chaotic, which proves the above conclusion that the root notching will cause some fluid migration to the hub. From Figure 26, it can be seen that the leakage fluid migrating to the hub enters the blade channel under the action of the flow direction pressure gradient and strong centrifugal force, and mixes with the low-energy fluid in the separation region of the boundary layer.  At about 50% chord length, it gradually curls into a radial vortex and moves higher. Combined with Figure 8, under the joint action of this radial vortex and the enlarged corner separation vortex, the flow loss of NRLE blades is enhanced, and the aerodynamic performance of the compressor is significantly reduced, but the stability margin is not significantly decreased compared to NTLE and NMLE

Finally, with the aim of investigating the cause behind the decrease in the stability margin of NRLE blades, Figure 26 presents a comparison of the three-dimensional flow lines between NRLE blades and normal blades under near stall conditions. By combining this with Figure 17(a) and (d), it can be observed that the presence of a notch at the root of the blade alters the flow direction of the incoming gas, causing it to move both upstream and downstream of the original flow path. This flow variation, induced by the notch damage, increases the adverse pressure gradient on the suction surface of the blade root in comparison to normal blades. Moreover
